HARD GUARDRAILS (violating any = failure):
- EDIT ONLY the champion repo /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch gate-s0-s5). NEVER edit /home/polaris/wt/flywheel (read-only reference).
- FAITHFULNESS FROZEN: never modify src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py or any strict_verify / citation-verification logic. READ ONLY. No new verification pass.
- Every new behavior behind a default-OFF flag (or a None-default kwarg) so the OFF path is BYTE-IDENTICAL to today's champion. Follow the repo's existing flag pattern.
- Additive & minimal. Prefer NEW modules over rewriting. Never invent a hard constraint that isn't from explicit prompt text (design rule).
- Scope must reach RETRIEVAL query generation — NEVER a post-fetch filter of a frozen corpus (the 997->131 anti-pattern).
- Do NOT run any full ~35-min live compose or RACE/FACT scoring (a workflow shell caps at 10 min). Use fast unit tests, OFFLINE compiles, BANKED replays, and query-gen TELEMETRY (no fetch) only. If a step needs a live run, BUILD + unit-test it and leave a documented harness; do not execute it.
- If a task needs a frozen/forbidden file, STOP and report instead.
SPEC DOCS (read both FIRST, in full):
- Consolidated design + build sequence: /home/polaris/polaris_project/GATE_DESIGN_CONSOLIDATED.md
- Sol's detailed hook map + schema: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/reviews/gate_gate_design.md
- (fallback if that path 404s) /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/reviews/sol_gate_design.md

IMPLEMENT S4 — Compose/render projections + compliance audit (BANKED/offline only).
- Compose: thread tone/audience/pov (from contract voice) into the section advisory-prose slot in src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py (_call_section / _select_section_system_prompt) — PROSE guidance only, no routing/verification change. document_type selects skeleton. Length = planning context, NEVER a truncation gate.
- Render: in scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:main thread deliverable_spec/scope_spec; contract-aware assembly (required sections/order, references dedup by work, tables from VERIFIED fields only). KEEP _audit_citations untouched. Record retrieval_scope_status='not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus' when run on a prebuilt corpus.
- Compliance audit: new src/polaris_graph/planning/contract_compliance.py:audit_contract(contract, report, outline, biblio) -> term-level SATISFIED/FAILED/UNSATISFIABLE/UNKNOWN + owning stage. Deterministic for counts/headings/length/tables/citations; a SEPARATE cheap judge only for semantic coverage. DISCLOSURE-ONLY — it must NEVER drop/edit content and NEVER touch strict_verify. Call it AFTER assembly, ALONGSIDE _audit_citations.
- Tests (offline/banked): audit_contract on a saved report produces a term-level report; required-section/order check is deterministic; faithfulness path untouched. OFF path byte-identical.
Report files + test results.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# The Research Planning Gate — Consolidated Design (Opus)
2	
3	**Consolidates two independent designs** (Sol / GPT-5.6 code-grounded, Fable 5) + a 2026 best-practices digest.
4	**Date:** 2026-07-16 · **Status:** DESIGN — build only after operator approval; build runs in a workflow, main session monitors.
5	**Target repo:** `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent` (champion `bot/outline-agent-box @ df4118a`). Never edit `flywheel/`.
6	
7	> **The thesis both designers reached independently:** the gate produces ONE durable, typed **research contract**, then COMPILES it into levers the champion pipeline *already exposes but the driver never populates* — retrieval-backend routing (`ResearchFrame`), FS-Researcher query text, and `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec`. **Scope always ADDS/ROUTES queries at retrieval; it NEVER filters a frozen corpus.** A disclosure-only compliance audit reads the same contract at the end. Faithfulness (`strict_verify`) is untouched.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## 1. Non-negotiables (carried from operator rulings)
12	1. **FAITHFULNESS FROZEN.** No edit to `src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py` / `strict_verify` / the drop rule. No new verification pass. A conformance test asserts a clean diff and that every recompose path still traverses the unchanged verifier.
13	2. **No starvation.** Scope reaches FS-Researcher *query generation* and backend routing BEFORE the first fetch. Any post-fetch filter is a secondary safety net for explicit prohibitions only — never the sole scope mechanism. (The 997→131 filter that tanked RACE 0.4447→0.3264 is the banned anti-pattern.)
14	3. **Never invent a constraint.** Enforced by TYPE: a hard/binding term requires `origin == explicit` (a real prompt span) or an affirmative user action. Inferred/default terms may only weight, prefer, or route — never hard-gate, exclude, or become a stated requirement.
15	4. **Priority COVERAGE > INSIGHT > READABILITY.** Length is planning context, never a truncation gate. Required coverage drives the gap ledger. Tone is prose-only.
16	5. **Fail-open.** Any gate error → empty/degraded contract → every lever reads its current default → byte-identical to today's champion. The gate can only ADD guardrails; it can never abort a run or drop a source.
17	6. **Reuse, don't rebuild.** Reuse the validated rule-reader (`round1-if-compiler:constraint_extractor.py`), the intake extractors, FS-Researcher, the ResearchFrame router, `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec`, the outline agent. New code is small and additive.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## 2. Architecture (both modes)
22	
23	```
24	caller passes mode EXPLICITLY (interactive | autonomous)   ← Sol: never infer mode from terminal/timeout
25	        │   benchmark driver ALWAYS passes autonomous
26	        ▼
27	[1 UNDERSTAND]  deterministic candidate extraction (rule-reader + intake extractors, exact spans)
28	        ▼
29	[2 COMPILE]     one structured LLM call → typed ResearchContract (every clause accounted for;
30	                each term tagged explicit/inferred/user + force hard/prefer/open)
31	        ▼
32	[3 VALIDATE+TRIAGE]  deterministic: span/quote equality, no inferred-hard, clause coverage, conflicts
33	        │                                    │
34	   INTERACTIVE                          AUTONOMOUS  (benchmark)
35	   show editable plan + diff            ask nobody, wait for nobody
36	   ask 0–3 MATERIAL questions           least-restrictive open defaults
37	   edit / answer / approve  ──┐         disclose assumptions; auto-pin      ← the two modes differ at ONLY this node
38	        ▼                     └──────────────┬─────────────┘
39	[4 PLAN+PIN]    2nd structured call → threads, query intents, outline seed, coverage matrix; hash+pin (immutable)
40	        ▼
41	[5 PROJECTIONS] same pinned artifact → retrieval | outline | compose | render   (typed per-stage views)
42	        ▼
43	[6 COMPLIANCE AUDIT]  term-level SATISFIED/FAILED/UNSATISFIABLE/UNKNOWN, routed to owning stage
44	                      (disclosure-only; strict_verify untouched)
45	```
46	
47	- **Interactive** (real users): ≤3 material questions; editable plan (Gemini-style natural-language edits); retrieval waits for approve/edit/answer/"use best judgment"; never crosses into autonomous on a timeout.
48	- **Autonomous** (benchmark; `PG_GATE_AUTONOMOUS=1`, the default in `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py`): a pure `contract→contract` function, **no I/O, never blocks**. Every would-ask becomes an open-ended assumption surfaced in report front-matter. *Enforced by a test asserting zero `needs_input` exits with no input channel.* This is the load-bearing mode — without it the gate can't be RACE-scored.
49	
50	---
51	
52	## 3. The contract schema (merged)
53	Adopt **Sol's typed governance skeleton** (it's the more complete one) with **Fable's `Tagged`-provenance discipline** on every term:
54	
55	- **Envelope:** immutable `PlanningGateArtifact` — `mode`, `state`, `original_prompt`, `contract`, `plan`, `clarification_questions`, `revisions` (JSON-Patch), `contract_sha256`/`plan_sha256`/`artifact_sha256`. Reuse the existing `research_planner.py:serialize_plan_canonical`/`plan_sha256`.
56	- **Every term = `ContractTerm{value, origin(explicit|user|inferred|policy_default), force(hard|prefer|open), spans[], rationale, enforcement_stages[]}`.** `explicit` requires exact `prompt[start:end]` quote equality. **Hard force is invalid unless origin==explicit/user** (the mechanical no-invention guarantee).
57	- **Groups:** `ObjectiveSpec` (question, purpose, audience, output_language, depth), `ScopeSpec` (source_types, quality, languages, date, geography/jurisdiction, domains, named_sources, prohibited), `ContentSpec` (required/optional `CoverageRequirement`s incl. comparisons/metrics/counterevidence + entities + breadth/depth), `DeliverableSpec` (kind, sections w/ exact-title & order locks, length, visuals, citation, rhetoric/tone), plus `ambiguities[]`, `assumptions[]`, `conflicts[]`, `complexity`.
58	- **Plan (separate, adapts to evidence while contract stays pinned):** `threads`, `evidence_needs`, `query_intents` (each carrying its scope term-ids + language/date/source lanes), `outline_seed`, **`coverage_matrix`** (every binding term → owning stage(s) + ≥1 retrieval lane), `budget` (`mandatory_lane_count`, overflow = expand-or-fail, **never silently truncate a mandatory lane**), `stop_conditions` (semantic sufficiency, not a source-count quota).
59	
60	---
61	
62	## 4. The understanding engine
63	A **bounded hybrid**, not one blind call (both agree; digest confirms):
64	1. **Deterministic candidate pass** — reuse `round1-if-compiler` `constraint_extractor` ⊕ champion `intake_constraint_extractor` (`extract_constraints_regex`, `extract_instruction_slots`, `extract_scope_constraints`). Preserve exact offsets. **Deterministic wins on overlap; LLM fills gaps.** One candidate adapter — one canonical contract, no competing truth sources.
65	2. **Contract-compiler call** — structured JSON; system prompt forbids marking implications as explicit, forbids inventing date/geo/source/length, requires open/null for unspecified, decomposes compound prompts preserving dependencies, records ambiguities + assumptions + conflicts, returns `clause_coverage` (every operative clause dispositioned). **Raise the current 2,000-token `plan_research` cap** — inadequate for compound contracts (Sol); use provider-resolved max + bounded 2-call structure.
66	3. **Deterministic validate + triage** — quote equality, no inferred-hard, every candidate represented-or-rejected, every clause dispositioned, conflicts surfaced. One correction retry → else conservative span-verified fallback (raw prompt as objective, explicit rules only, open elsewhere; `compiler_degraded=true`).
67	4. **Plan-compiler call** — from the *validated contract*: smallest complete thread set (scale to complexity, no fixed quota), every evidence-bearing requirement gets ≥1 mandatory query intent, scope encoded in query intents (hard→query+backend filter, soft→ranking), coverage matrix built, exact headings only from explicit instruction.
68	
69	Cost: 2 small-model calls (glm-5.2, same as FS query-gen) — negligible vs retrieval+outline.
70	
71	---
72	
73	## 5. Per-stage enforcement (merged hook map)
74	
75	| Stage | What the contract does | Champion hook (verified) |
76	|---|---|---|
77	| **Gate entry** | compile/load artifact, require approved/auto_pinned, persist `planning_gate_artifact.json` | new `planning/research_planning_gate.py:run_research_planning_gate`; in `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:run_one_query` replace the raw `plan_research(_clean_question,…)` decision |
78	| **Retrieval (the no-starvation core)** | `source_types→evidence_needs` routes scholarly backends (S2/OpenAlex) = **GO FIND journals**; scope terms + languages + entities into query TEXT; hard scope → backend-native filter, soft → ranking | `live_retriever.py:run_live_retrieval` (accepts `research_frame`/`protocol`/`amplified_queries`); `fs_researcher_query_gen.py:_plan_expert_facet_queries` + `_multilingual_native_reserve` + `sub_entity_query_expander`; `expert_facet_planner._question_anchor`. **FS branch in `run_one_query` currently passes only `_clean_question` and discards `_research_plan.sub_queries` — fix that.** |
79	| **Outline (FEED)** | seed required sections + pre-load `required_coverage`/`must_address` as PENDING gaps so the deep-think loop CLOSES contract gaps; thread scope into gap search | `outline_agent.py:run_outline_agent_or_legacy` (already takes `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec` → `outline_digest.build_requirements_block`). **BUG (both found): `_tool_search_more_evidence` drops `protocol`/`research_frame` → deep-think search runs unscoped. Thread scope through `OutlineWorkspace`.** (accepts `**_ignored` today — clean seam) |
80	| **Compose** | tone/audience/pov into the section advisory-prose slot (prose only); document_type selects skeleton | `multi_section_generator.py:generate_multi_section_report` (:~3010 seams), `_call_section`, `_select_section_system_prompt`. Length = planning context, never truncation. |
81	| **Render** | required sections/order, tables from VERIFIED fields only, references dedup by work, length reported | `compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:main` assembly — thread specs; keep `_audit_citations`. Mark `retrieval_scope_status` when run on a prebuilt corpus. |
82	| **Compliance audit** | term-level satisfied/failed/unsatisfiable/unknown + owning stage; deterministic where possible, one cheap judge for semantic coverage | new `planning/contract_compliance.py:audit_contract`, run AFTER assembly ALONGSIDE (never touching) the frozen faithfulness tripwire. Disclosure-only. |
83	
84	---
85	
86	## 6. Gate ↔ outline-agent seam: **FEED** (both agree)
87	Gate replaces the *pre-retrieval* planning call; the outline agent stays the *post-retrieval evidence-aware refiner*. Refactor `run_outline_agent_or_legacy` into `refine_outline_from_seed(seed_outline, contract, retrieval_projection, coverage_matrix, …)` with the legacy path as `seed = supplied_seed or legacy_call_outline(...)`. Seed behavior depends on provenance: explicit/user headings = immutable title+order; gate-proposed headings = stable `section_id` + revisable title, splittable/mergeable only if every binding term stays mapped. **Required topics are coverage obligations, NOT automatically headings** (the round1 mistake). Extend `OutlineWorkspace` with the contract hash + term ledger; validate every `update_outline` against it (reject dropping an explicit lock or the last owner of a binding term). Degrades to exact champion behavior when the contract names no structure.
88	
89	---
90	
91	## 7. Ask-vs-assume (merged policy)
92	Ask **iff uncertain AND consequential**, ≤3, interactive only. Eligible only if: not already answered, ≥2 plausible interpretations remain, the choice materially changes discovery/structure/decision, and open-leaving wastes real research. Autonomous = **zero asks**, least-restrictive open defaults, every assumption disclosed. Locked defaults: no hard date cutoff ever (prefer-fresher soft, per operator); geography/jurisdiction stay open/comparative; source-type open+prefer-authoritative unless explicit; source-language ≠ prompt language; tone/heading never trigger a question. Explicit "only/must/exclude/do not use" + supplied ranges/counts/headings = hard; "prefer/focus/especially" = soft. When a soft preference starves a mandatory thread → relax + disclose; when two explicit terms conflict → interactive asks, autonomous executes the feasible intersection and labels the rest `UNSATISFIABLE`.
93	
94	---
95	
96	## 8. Build sequence (spikes first; each gated; faithfulness frozen throughout)
97	Work on a fresh branch off `bot/outline-agent-box`. Every step additive + flag-gated (OFF = byte-identical) + measured.
98	
99	- **S0 — Port + reconcile (no behavior change).** Port `round1` rule-reader + tests into champion; put it and the intake extractors behind ONE candidate adapter. Do NOT port the compose-time `filter_eligible` wiring or the coverage-to-heading mapping. *Accept:* candidate reconciliation corpus; OFF path byte-identical.
100	- **S1 — Contract + plan schema + compiler (offline).** Schema, 2 structured calls, deterministic validators. *Accept:* compile all 100 DRB prompts in autonomous mode; human-audit a stratified sample — **no invented hard constraint, no missing explicit clause, an assumption record for every inferred term** (not merely valid JSON).
101	- **S2 — Retrieval projection (the no-starvation proof).** Thread `retrieval_plan`/`research_frame` into FS-Researcher + `run_live_retrieval`; scope into query text + backend routing. *Accept:* on a FRESH task-72 run, telemetry proves journal/English + every mandatory topic reach `_plan_expert_facet_queries` BEFORE results exist; the gate never reduces evidence count vs no-gate (the mechanized 997-row guard).
102	- **S3 — Outline FEED + gap-scope fix.** `refine_outline_from_seed`; seed gap ledger; fix `_tool_search_more_evidence` scope drop. *Accept:* replay banked corpora (tasks 30/61/76/90) — real refinement, locks + term-mappings preserved, gap queries stay in-scope, no second fresh planner.
103	- **S4 — Compose/render projections + compliance audit.** Thread specs; deterministic renderer; `audit_contract`. *Accept:* exact section order / comparison table / length range all trace to verified claims; term-level audit produced.
104	- **S5 — End-to-end fresh autonomous run** on tasks {4,30,61,72,76,90}: retrieval→outline→compose→render, RACE + FACT, **3× per the measurement protocol**. *Accept:* RACE (esp. Instruction-Following) up vs champion, **FACT ≥ 90.3% held**, `provenance_generator.py` clean diff.
105	
106	**Release gate is behavioral** (both insist): the contract must visibly alter FS discovery, survive outline refinement, constrain compose/render, and yield term-level audit results on REAL output. Green unit tests / a filtered fixed corpus / a source-count bump do NOT prove the gate fired.
107	
108	---
109	
110	## 9. Top risks (both designers) + mitigation
111	- **Hallucinated hard constraint** → the `origin==explicit`-for-hard type rule + clause coverage + assumption list.
112	- **Re-introducing the filter trap** → design has NO post-fetch scope filter path; mechanized "gate never lowers evidence count" test.
113	- **Scope loss in outline gap search** → the `_tool_search_more_evidence` fix (both found this bug).
114	- **Two-planner fight** → FEED + required-title subordination already in `apply_revision_ops`.
115	- **Budget truncating a mandatory lane** → compute `mandatory_lane_count` first; expand or fail loudly.
116	- **Compliance-judge circularity** → deterministic checks for counts/headings/length/tables/citations; a separately-configured judge only for semantic coverage; it can NEVER alter faithfulness verdicts.
117	- **Autonomous blocking** → STAGE-2 autonomous is pure `contract→contract`; test asserts zero `needs_input` with no input channel.
118	
119	---
120	
121	*Full independent designs preserved at:* `scratchpad/reviews/sol_gate_design.md` (Sol) and the Fable agent transcript. *Best-practices digest:* `scratchpad/reviews/gate_bestpractices_digest.md`.
122

content.content:
1	## 1. The gate architecture (diagram + stages), interactive AND autonomous.
2	
3	The Research Planning Gate is a pre-retrieval compiler, not another report writer and not a filter over whatever evidence happens to have been retrieved. Its unit of work is a versioned, immutable `PlanningGateArtifact`: the original request, a typed research contract, a research plan, every explicit assumption or unresolved ambiguity, and stage-specific projections. All expensive stages consume the same pinned artifact and report compliance against its stable term IDs.
4	
5	```text
6	                    caller sets mode explicitly
7	                 ┌──────────────────────────────┐
8	                 │ raw request + run context    │
9	                 │ INTERACTIVE | AUTONOMOUS     │
10	                 └──────────────┬───────────────┘
11	                                v
12	      ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
13	      │ 1. deterministic candidate extraction            │
14	      │    rule-reader + intake/scope/slot extractors     │
15	      └───────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘

28	                   v                         v

30	      │ show draft plan + diff │  │ ask nobody; wait for nobody│
31	      │ ask 0–3 material Qs    │  │ use broad/open defaults    │
32	      │ edit / answer / approve│  │ disclose assumptions       │
33	      └────────────┬───────────┘  │ auto-pin policy revision   │

47	             v          v          v          v
48	       FS-Researcher  evidence-   verified   deterministic
49	       + live search  aware       section    rendering

51	             └──────────┴──────────┴──────────┘
52	                          v
53	      ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
54	      │ 6. contract compliance audit                     │

58	```
59	
60	The stages are deliberately split as follows.
61	
62	**Admission and mode selection.** The caller must pass `mode: "interactive" | "autonomous"`; the gate must not infer mode from a terminal, timeout, environment accident, or presence of a UI. The benchmark driver always passes `autonomous`. A product UI normally passes `interactive`. This prevents the benchmark from blocking forever on a clarification that no human can answer, and prevents an interactive request from silently switching to assumptions because a timer expired.
63	
64	**Candidate extraction.** Existing deterministic readers identify candidate constraints and their exact prompt spans before an LLM interprets the request. Port the validated `round1-if-compiler:src/polaris_graph/instruction/constraint_extractor.py` implementation and its tests into the champion, then reconcile it behind one adapter with `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/intake_constraint_extractor.py:extract_user_constraints`, `extract_instruction_slots`, and `extract_scope_constraints`. This is reuse, not a second independent truth source: candidates flow into the compiler and validators; the pinned contract is the sole downstream authority. The rejected branch behavior—filtering a prebuilt corpus at compose time—is not ported.
65	
66	**Contract compilation.** A structured reasoning call turns candidates and the raw request into a normalized contract. It must account for every clause, including nested questions, parentheticals, format requests, source restrictions, and exclusions. Each contract term carries origin and evidence. An item may be semantically inferred as a useful research direction, but an inferred item may never become a hard retrieval exclusion or an invented user requirement.
67	
68	**Validation and triage.** Deterministic validators reject unsupported `EXPLICIT` labels, inferred hard constraints, missing required-clause mappings, contradictory locks, invalid date ranges, and orphaned requirements. A bounded correction call receives machine-readable errors. Ambiguities are represented rather than erased.
69	
70	**Interactive branch.** The user sees the draft contract and plan as an editable artifact, plus a compact diff between their text and the normalized interpretation. The gate asks zero to three questions only when an answer can materially change retrieval or the deliverable. Retrieval does not begin until the user approves, edits, or explicitly selects “use best judgment.” Edits are recompiled, validated, and shown as a revision; approval pins the exact revision and hash.
71	
72	**Autonomous branch.** This is the mandatory benchmark path. It never emits a blocking question and never waits for approval. It resolves material ambiguity with the least restrictive defensible interpretation, keeps unspecified dimensions open, records every assumption, and auto-pins the revision under a named policy version. “No date stated” therefore means an open date range with a soft preference for fresher evidence where relevance is equal—not a fabricated five-year cutoff. An unresolvable contradiction is recorded as such; the system either continues on the non-conflicting subset with an explicit limitation or returns `UNSATISFIABLE` when the requested result is logically impossible. It must not guess a jurisdiction, population, source type, date window, or exact length simply to fill a schema field.
73	
74	**Plan compilation and pinning.** A second structured call decomposes the validated contract into research threads, query intents, and an outline seed. A deterministic compiler creates a coverage matrix from every binding contract term to at least one owning stage and, where evidence is required, at least one retrieval lane. The plan scales to the request: four to six directions are a useful common shape, not a fixed quota. Compound prompts may need more; narrow prompts may need fewer. Query caps may not silently erase mandatory threads. The canonical JSON and SHA-256 approach already present in `src/polaris_graph/planning/research_planner.py:serialize_plan_canonical` and `plan_sha256` should be retained for the richer artifact.
75	
76	**Execution and revision.** The pinned artifact is immutable. Evidence-aware outline refinement may update an execution plan, but it cannot mutate the contract. A legitimate later user change creates a new artifact revision, records a JSON Patch and reason, supersedes the old hash, and invalidates affected downstream checkpoints. Autonomous gap discovery can revise inferred query tactics without changing explicit terms; those tactical revisions are also logged.
77	
78	**Compliance.** Checkpoints audit the contract after retrieval, outline, composition, and render. A final audit reports each term as `SATISFIED`, `FAILED`, `UNSATISFIABLE`, `UNKNOWN`, or `NOT_APPLICABLE`, with output/evidence spans and the responsible stage. Compliance is distinct from factual faithfulness. `src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:strict_verify` and the surrounding provenance engine are frozen: no gate change, remediation rule, or format transformation may weaken, bypass, or edit them.
79	
80	Within the space of faithful outputs, execution preserves the champion objective ordering: coverage before additional insight, and additional insight before readability polish. That ordering never licenses an unsupported claim; frozen faithfulness is an invariant, not a tradeable score.
81	
82	The primary paid-run integration point is `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:run_one_query`, before either standard or FS retrieval. `scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:main` starts from a frozen corpus; it can load or autonomously compile a gate artifact to exercise outline/compose/render behavior, but it must label retrieval-scope enforcement as unavailable. Wiring only that script would repeat the known starvation error because no gate scope could influence discovery.
83	
84	## 2. The contract schema (full, typed).

86	Use versioned Pydantic/dataclass models with strict parsing (`extra="forbid"`) and canonical serialization. The following is the logical schema; implementation can split it across `planning_gate_schema.py` and `research_planning_gate.py`.

362	`stop_conditions` express semantic sufficiency—every mandatory thread has usable evidence of the required kind, explicit comparisons have evidence on each side, requested counterevidence was sought, and no unresolved high-impact gap remains. They supplement the champion’s saturation logic; a raw source count cannot substitute for term coverage.

401	Every source of interpretation remains distinguishable. `explicit` requires a valid prompt span; `user_answer` and `user_edit` are authoritative but retain their revision record; `inferred` and `policy_default` must appear in `assumptions`. Hard requirements may originate only from explicit text or an affirmative user action. A compiler can infer a subquestion or query synonym, but it cannot infer “English-only,” “peer-reviewed-only,” “United States,” “last five years,” or “2,000 words” as a hard constraint.
402	
403	The edit flow supports both a form/JSON Patch and a natural-language edit. Natural-language edits pass through the same extractor, compiler, and validator, with the previous artifact supplied for a precise diff. A user may edit scope, required coverage, structure, or rhetoric; the UI shows which retrieval lanes and downstream sections would change. Approval pins a complete artifact, not a prose summary whose interpretation could drift later.
404	
405	For compatibility, provide a lossless-enough adapter from this plan to the existing `src/polaris_graph/planning/research_planner.py:ResearchPlan` (`ResearchFrame`, flattened `sub_queries`, and `SectionOutlineItem`). New paths consume the rich artifact directly. The adapter is transitional: fields such as term provenance, hard/soft force, visual requirements, and coverage bindings must not be discarded at the FS or generator boundary.
406	
407	## 3. The understanding engine (decomposition/clarification/inference; prompt + output schema).
408	
409	The understanding engine is a bounded hybrid pipeline rather than a single blind planner call.
410	
411	**Candidate pass.** Run the ported validated rule-reader and the champion’s three intake extractors. Preserve exact character offsets. Normalize aliases (for example, “academic papers” versus “journal articles”) without changing force. Extract verbs and coordination structure so “compare A and B on X and Y, then recommend” becomes comparison requirements for both entities and both dimensions plus a synthesis requirement—not a bag of keywords. Detect requested output objects such as a top-ten table, mind map, legal case analysis, forecast, or literature review. Candidate extraction is high-recall; it does not itself pin a contract.
412	
413	**Contract compiler call.** Use the configured planner role through the repository’s model resolver, maximum supported reasoning effort, and the serving provider’s real maximum completion cap. The current `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:run_one_query` bridge to `plan_research` caps output at 2,000 tokens; that cap is inadequate for arbitrary compound contracts. Control cost with two bounded structured calls and strict schemas, not by starving reasoning or output.
414	
415	Recommended compiler system prompt:
416	
417	```text
418	You are the POLARIS Research Contract Compiler. Convert the user's complete request
419	into the supplied ResearchContract JSON schema. Your output is a contract for a
420	research system, not the answer to the research question.
421	
422	Rules:
423	1. Account for every imperative, question, entity, comparison dimension, scope phrase,
424	   source rule, exclusion, format request, and rhetorical instruction in the request.
425	2. A term marked origin="explicit" MUST include exact character spans copied from the
426	   request. Do not mark implications or world knowledge as explicit.
427	3. Never invent a restriction. If date, geography, jurisdiction, source type, source
428	   language, length, audience, or format is unspecified, represent it as open/null.
429	4. Inferred and policy-default terms may only be preference/open, never hard. List every
430	   such interpretation in assumptions and explain its consequence.
431	5. Distinguish source language from output language. Distinguish required topics from
432	   required section headings. Distinguish content scope from presentation shape.
433	6. Decompose compound and nested questions without dropping qualifiers. Preserve
434	   dependencies (for example, evidence first, comparison second, recommendation last).
435	7. Identify ambiguity rather than resolving it invisibly. Mark whether it can safely
436	   remain open and which execution stages it changes.
437	8. Preserve conflicting explicit instructions in conflicts; do not silently choose one.
438	9. Do not set source-count, date-window, jurisdiction, length, or section-count quotas
439	   unless the user supplied them.
440	10. Return JSON only, conforming exactly to the schema. Do not research or answer.
441	
442	INPUT:
443	- current UTC date (context only, not a user constraint)
444	- mode
445	- exact user request
446	- deterministic candidates with spans
447	- named product defaults and their policy IDs
448	```
449	
450	The compiler output is the `ResearchContract` schema from section 2 plus a `clause_coverage` array:
451	
452	```json
453	{
454	  "contract": { "...": "ResearchContract" },
455	  "clause_coverage": [
456	    {
457	      "span": {"start": 0, "end": 18, "quote": "..."},
458	      "term_ids": ["ct_content_001"],
459	      "disposition": "represented|non_instructional|ambiguous",
460	      "explanation": "..."
461	    }
462	  ]
463	}
464	```
465	
466	**Contract validation.** Validate the JSON schema, offsets, quote equality, enum normalization, date order, stable IDs, and force/origin invariants. Then perform semantic checks: every deterministic candidate is represented or explicitly rejected with a reason; every operative prompt clause has a disposition; each assumption points to a non-explicit term; each hard term is user-supported; required item counts are preserved; and mutually exclusive requirements are surfaced. The validator returns structured errors. One correction call receives only the original request, rejected JSON, and errors. A second invalid result falls back in autonomous mode to a conservative deterministic contract containing the full raw request as the primary objective, span-verified explicit rules only, and open scope elsewhere; it records `compiler_degraded=true`. Interactive mode shows the conservative draft and asks only if the missing interpretation is material.
467	
468	**Ambiguity triage.** Generate question candidates from `Ambiguity` records, not from a generic desire for more detail. A question is eligible only when the prompt does not already answer it, at least two plausible interpretations remain, the choice materially changes retrieval/structure/decision utility, and leaving it open has meaningful expected regret. Deduplicate coupled ambiguities into one question. In interactive mode choose at most three material questions and show their consequence. In autonomous mode generate no user-facing questions; apply the policy in section 6 and keep the assumption visible.
469	
470	**Plan compiler call.** The validated contract—not the raw prompt alone—is the input. Recommended system prompt:
471	
472	```text
473	You are the POLARIS Research Plan Compiler. Produce ResearchExecutionPlan JSON for the
474	pinned ResearchContract. Do not answer the research question and do not change the contract.
475	
476	Rules:
477	1. Create the smallest complete set of research threads that covers every binding content
478	   requirement; scale with complexity rather than targeting a fixed number.
479	2. Preserve dependencies: descriptive/baseline evidence precedes comparison, causal or
480	   correlational analysis, forecast, and recommendation where applicable.
481	3. Give every requirement needing evidence at least one mandatory query intent. Add distinct
482	   lanes when source type, language, jurisdiction, date, named source, primary-source status,
483	   comparison side, or counterevidence requires a different discovery strategy.
484	4. Scope must shape discovery. Encode explicit hard scope in query intents and backend-filter
485	   projections; encode soft/inferred scope as ranking preferences. Never plan to satisfy scope
486	   solely by filtering a fixed corpus after retrieval.
487	5. Do not drop a mandatory lane to meet a query cap. Report mandatory_lane_count and use the
488	   budget overflow policy.
489	6. Map every contract term to its owning stage(s), research thread(s), query intent(s) when
490	   evidence is needed, outline section(s) when presentation is needed, and audit method.
491	7. Required topics are not automatically headings. Create exact heading locks only from an
492	   explicit/user-approved section instruction. Otherwise use stable semantic section IDs and
493	   revisable display titles.
494	8. Treat early outline structure as a seed for evidence-aware refinement, not a factual claim
495	   that evidence exists. Define semantic sufficiency and gap conditions without inventing source
496	   counts.
497	9. Return JSON only and conform exactly to ResearchExecutionPlan.
498	```
499	
500	**Plan validation.** Compile a bipartite coverage graph and fail the plan if any binding content term has no thread, any evidence-bearing requirement has no mandatory query intent, any explicit comparison omits a side or dimension, any required deliverable element has no stage owner, any exact section lock is absent, or any mandatory lane can be silently truncated by `max_queries`. Validate that retrieval query intents carry the term IDs for all applicable source/date/language/geography/jurisdiction constraints. One structured correction retry is sufficient; otherwise use a deterministic one-thread-per-required-clause plan rather than dropping clauses.
501	
502	**Approval presentation.** The human-facing draft is generated deterministically from the typed data, not by another summarizer that can omit terms. It shows: “What I understand,” scope (required/preferred/open), questions to answer, proposed structure, deliverable/rhetoric, assumptions, conflicts, and a term-to-stage trace. Users can approve all, edit a field, answer a question, or use best judgment. The autonomous artifact stores the same view for forensic inspection even though no UI is shown.
503	
504	**No plan drift.** All stage prompts include the artifact hash and only their typed projection. A stage may report a proposed tactical amendment, but it cannot rewrite the canonical contract in memory. Logs and raw LLM I/O record the artifact revision and term IDs. This makes a final failure traceable to extraction, planning, retrieval, outline, compose, or render rather than to an unobservable prompt reinterpretation.
505	
506	## 4. Per-stage enforcement map (retrieval→outline→compose→render→compliance audit) with file:function hooks.
