1005	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
1006	        return {
1007	            "plan_version": self.plan_version,
1008	            "threads": [t.to_dict() for t in self.threads],
1009	            "evidence_needs": [e.to_dict() for e in self.evidence_needs],
1010	            "query_intents": [q.to_dict() for q in self.query_intents],
1011	            "outline_seed": [o.to_dict() for o in self.outline_seed],
1012	            "coverage_matrix": [c.to_dict() for c in self.coverage_matrix],
1013	            "budget": self.budget.to_dict(),
1014	            "stop_conditions": list(self.stop_conditions),
1015	        }
1016	
1017	
1018	def plan_from_dict(d: Any) -> ResearchExecutionPlan:
1019	    if not isinstance(d, dict):
1020	        raise SchemaError(f"plan must be an object, got {type(d).__name__}")
1021	    return ResearchExecutionPlan(
1022	        plan_version=_as_opt_str(d.get("plan_version")) or SCHEMA_VERSION,
1023	        threads=[ResearchThread.from_dict(t) for t in (d.get("threads") or []) if isinstance(t, dict)],
1024	        evidence_needs=[EvidenceNeed.from_dict(e) for e in (d.get("evidence_needs") or []) if isinstance(e, dict)],
1025	        query_intents=[QueryIntent.from_dict(q) for q in (d.get("query_intents") or []) if isinstance(q, dict)],
1026	        outline_seed=[OutlineSeed.from_dict(o) for o in (d.get("outline_seed") or []) if isinstance(o, dict)],
1027	        coverage_matrix=[CoverageBinding.from_dict(c) for c in (d.get("coverage_matrix") or []) if isinstance(c, dict)],
1028	        budget=BudgetEnvelope.from_dict(d.get("budget") or {}),
1029	        stop_conditions=_as_str_list(d.get("stop_conditions")),
1030	    )
1031	
1032	
1033	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1034	# The persisted envelope
1035	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1036	
1037	@dataclass
1038	class ClarificationQuestion:
1039	    question_id: str
1040	    prompt: str
1041	    choices: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
1042	    affected_term_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
1043	    why_it_matters: str = ""
1044	    answer: Optional[str] = None
1045	
1046	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
1047	        return {
1048	            "question_id": self.question_id,
1049	            "prompt": self.prompt,
1050	            "choices": list(self.choices),
1051	            "affected_term_ids": list(self.affected_term_ids),
1052	            "why_it_matters": self.why_it_matters,
1053	            "answer": self.answer,
1054	        }
1055	
1056	
1057	@dataclass
1058	class ArtifactRevision:
1059	    revision: int
1060	    parent_sha256: Optional[str] = None
1061	    patch: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
1062	    actor: str = "compiler"
1063	    reason: str = ""
1064	
1065	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
1066	        return {
1067	            "revision": self.revision,
1068	            "parent_sha256": self.parent_sha256,
1069	            "patch": list(self.patch),
1070	            "actor": self.actor,
1071	            "reason": self.reason,
1072	        }
1073	
1074	
1075	@dataclass
1076	class PlanningGateArtifact:
1077	    """The immutable persisted envelope every downstream stage consumes."""
1078	
1079	    run_id: str
1080	    mode: str
1081	    state: str
1082	    original_prompt: str
1083	    contract: ResearchContract
1084	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan
1085	    artifact_version: str = SCHEMA_VERSION
1086	    created_at: str = ""
1087	    normalized_prompt_language: Optional[str] = None
1088	    clarification_questions: list[ClarificationQuestion] = field(default_factory=list)
1089	    revisions: list[ArtifactRevision] = field(default_factory=list)
1090	    approval_actor: Optional[str] = None
1091	    approval_policy_version: Optional[str] = None
1092	    contract_sha256: str = ""
1093	    plan_sha256: str = ""
1094	    artifact_sha256: str = ""
1095	
1096	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
1097	        return {
1098	            "artifact_version": self.artifact_version,
1099	            "run_id": self.run_id,
1100	            "created_at": self.created_at,
1101	            "mode": self.mode,
1102	            "state": self.state,
1103	            "original_prompt": self.original_prompt,
1104	            "normalized_prompt_language": self.normalized_prompt_language,
1105	            "contract": self.contract.to_dict(),
1106	            "plan": self.plan.to_dict(),
1107	            "clarification_questions": [q.to_dict() for q in self.clarification_questions],
1108	            "revisions": [r.to_dict() for r in self.revisions],
1109	            "approval_actor": self.approval_actor,
1110	            "approval_policy_version": self.approval_policy_version,
1111	            "contract_sha256": self.contract_sha256,
1112	            "plan_sha256": self.plan_sha256,
1113	            "artifact_sha256": self.artifact_sha256,
1114	        }
1115	
1116	    def recompute_hashes(self) -> "PlanningGateArtifact":
1117	        """Fill in the three SHAs from the canonical bytes (idempotent)."""
1118	        self.contract_sha256 = sha256_of(self.contract.to_dict())
1119	        self.plan_sha256 = sha256_of(self.plan.to_dict())
1120	        envelope = self.to_dict()
1121	        envelope["artifact_sha256"] = ""  # hash excludes itself
1122	        self.artifact_sha256 = sha256_of(envelope)
1123	        return self
1124	
1125	
1126	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1127	# Canonical serialization + hashing (mirrors research_planner discipline)
1128	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1129	
1130	def serialize_canonical(obj: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
1131	    """Canonical JSON: ``sort_keys`` + fixed separators + ``ensure_ascii=False``.
1132	
1133	    Byte-for-byte identical construction to
1134	    ``research_planner.serialize_plan_canonical`` so the two share one hashing
1135	    discipline.
1136	    """
1137	    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False)
1138	
1139	
1140	def sha256_of(obj: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
1141	    """SHA-256 of an object's canonical-JSON bytes."""
1142	    return hashlib.sha256(serialize_canonical(obj).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
1143	
1144	
1145	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1146	# Deterministic validators — the mechanical no-invention gate
1147	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1148	
1149	@dataclass
1150	class ValidationError:
1151	    """One machine-readable validation failure (fed to the correction retry)."""
1152	
1153	    code: str
1154	    message: str
1155	    term_id: Optional[str] = None
1156	    span: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
1157	
1158	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
1159	        return {
1160	            "code": self.code,
1161	            "message": self.message,
1162	            "term_id": self.term_id,
1163	            "span": self.span,
1164	        }
1165	
1166	
1167	def validate_contract(contract: ResearchContract, prompt: str) -> list[ValidationError]:
1168	    """Deterministic contract validation. Returns a (possibly empty) error list.
1169	
1170	    NEVER raises for a correctness problem — it accumulates structured errors so
1171	    the compiler can run one bounded correction retry, and so tests can assert on
1172	    specific codes. The checks (design §3 / §4):
1173	
1174	    * ``hard_not_explicit`` — a ``force==hard`` term whose ``origin`` is not
1175	      explicit/user_answer/user_edit (the no-invention invariant).
1176	    * ``explicit_without_span`` — an ``origin==explicit`` term with no span.
1177	    * ``span_quote_mismatch`` — an explicit term's span where
1178	      ``prompt[start:end] != quote``.
1179	    * ``bad_origin`` / ``bad_force`` / ``bad_stage`` — out-of-vocab enum leaked in.
1180	    * ``inferred_not_disclosed`` — an inferred/policy_default term with a value
1181	      but no matching :class:`Assumption` (every inferred term must be disclosed).
1182	    * ``duplicate_term_id`` — two terms share a ``term_id`` (IDs must be stable
1183	      and unique for downstream projection).
1184	    """
1185	    errors: list[ValidationError] = []
1186	    prompt = prompt or ""
1187	
1188	    seen_ids: set[str] = set()
1189	    disclosed: set[str] = set()
1190	    for a in contract.assumptions:
1191	        disclosed.update(a.affected_term_ids)
1192	
1193	    for term in contract.all_terms():
1194	        tid = term.term_id or "<anon>"
1195	
1196	        if term.term_id:
1197	            if term.term_id in seen_ids:
1198	                errors.append(ValidationError(
1199	                    "duplicate_term_id",
1200	                    f"term_id {term.term_id!r} appears more than once",
1201	                    term_id=term.term_id,
1202	                ))
1203	            seen_ids.add(term.term_id)
1204	
1205	        if term.origin not in ORIGINS:
1206	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1207	                "bad_origin", f"unknown origin {term.origin!r}", term_id=tid))
1208	        if term.force not in FORCES:
1209	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1210	                "bad_force", f"unknown force {term.force!r}", term_id=tid))
1211	        for st in term.enforcement_stages:
1212	            if st not in STAGES:
1213	                errors.append(ValidationError(
1214	                    "bad_stage", f"unknown enforcement stage {st!r}", term_id=tid))
1215	
1216	        # THE no-invention invariant: hard requires an authoritative origin.
1217	        if term.is_hard() and term.origin not in HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS:
1218	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1219	                "hard_not_explicit",
1220	                f"term {tid!r} is force=hard but origin={term.origin!r}; a hard "
1221	                f"term requires origin in {sorted(HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS)}",
1222	                term_id=tid,
1223	            ))
1224	
1225	        # explicit ⇒ at least one span, and every span must quote-match.
1226	        if term.origin == ORIGIN_EXPLICIT:
1227	            if not term.spans:
1228	                errors.append(ValidationError(
1229	                    "explicit_without_span",
1230	                    f"term {tid!r} is origin=explicit but carries no prompt span",
1231	                    term_id=tid,
1232	                ))
1233	            for sp in term.spans:
1234	                if not sp.matches_prompt(prompt):
1235	                    errors.append(ValidationError(
1236	                        "span_quote_mismatch",
1237	                        f"term {tid!r} span quote != prompt[{sp.start}:{sp.end}]",
1238	                        term_id=tid,
1239	                        span=sp.to_dict(),
1240	                    ))
1241	
1242	        # inferred/policy_default with a real value must be disclosed.
1243	        if (
1244	            term.origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS
1245	            and term.value not in (None, "", [], {})
1246	            and term.term_id
1247	            and term.term_id not in disclosed
1248	        ):
1249	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1250	                "inferred_not_disclosed",
1251	                f"term {tid!r} is origin={term.origin!r} with a value but has no "
1252	                f"assumption record disclosing it",
1253	                term_id=tid,
1254	            ))
1255	
1256	    return errors
1257	
1258	
1259	def validate_plan(
1260	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan, contract: ResearchContract
1261	) -> list[ValidationError]:
1262	    """Deterministic plan validation (design §4 plan-validation).
1263	
1264	    * ``requirement_without_intent`` — a required, evidence-bearing coverage
1265	      requirement with no mandatory query intent referencing it (via a thread).
1266	    * ``mandatory_lane_truncatable`` — ``budget.max_queries`` below
1267	      ``mandatory_lane_count`` under an ``expand``-or-``fail`` overflow policy is
1268	      only OK if the policy is not silent truncation; a smaller cap with a bad
1269	      policy is flagged.
1270	    * ``binding_term_unowned`` — a hard contract term with no coverage binding.
1271	    """
1272	    errors: list[ValidationError] = []
1273	
1274	    intent_ids_by_thread: dict[str, list[QueryIntent]] = {}
1275	    for qi in plan.query_intents:
1276	        intent_ids_by_thread.setdefault(qi.thread_id, []).append(qi)
1277	
1278	    covered_req_ids: set[str] = set()
1279	    for th in plan.threads:
1280	        has_mandatory_intent = any(
1281	            qi.mandatory for qi in intent_ids_by_thread.get(th.thread_id, [])
1282	        )
1283	        if has_mandatory_intent:
1284	            covered_req_ids.update(th.coverage_requirement_ids)
1285	
1286	    for cr in contract.coverage:
1287	        if not cr.required:
1288	            continue
1289	        if cr.requirement_id and cr.requirement_id not in covered_req_ids:
1290	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1291	                "requirement_without_intent",
1292	                f"required coverage {cr.requirement_id!r} has no mandatory query "
1293	                f"intent (via a thread)",
1294	                term_id=cr.requirement_id,
1295	            ))
1296	
1297	    bound_term_ids = {cb.contract_term_id for cb in plan.coverage_matrix}
1298	    for term in contract.hard_terms():
1299	        if term.term_id and term.term_id not in bound_term_ids:
1300	            errors.append(ValidationError(
1301	                "binding_term_unowned",
1302	                f"hard term {term.term_id!r} has no coverage binding / owning stage",
1303	                term_id=term.term_id,
1304	            ))
1305	
1306	    b = plan.budget
1307	    mandatory = sum(1 for qi in plan.query_intents if qi.mandatory)
1308	    if b.mandatory_lane_count and mandatory and b.mandatory_lane_count != mandatory:
1309	        # advisory: recorded lane count should match the actual mandatory intents
1310	        errors.append(ValidationError(
1311	            "mandatory_lane_count_mismatch",
1312	            f"budget.mandatory_lane_count={b.mandatory_lane_count} but "
1313	            f"{mandatory} mandatory query intents exist",
1314	        ))
1315	    if (
1316	        b.max_queries is not None
1317	        and b.mandatory_lane_count
1318	        and b.max_queries < b.mandatory_lane_count
1319	        and b.overflow_policy != "fail_preflight"
1320	    ):
1321	        errors.append(ValidationError(
1322	            "mandatory_lane_truncatable",
1323	            f"max_queries={b.max_queries} < mandatory_lane_count="
1324	            f"{b.mandatory_lane_count} under overflow_policy={b.overflow_policy!r}; "
1325	            f"a mandatory lane could be silently truncated",
1326	        ))
1327	
1328	    return errors
1329

content.text: Let me look at the flag module more, and the existing S2/S3 projection modules and outline_gate_feed to understand established patterns for the flag idiom and projection builders.

content.content:
1	"""Retrieval projection (S2) — the no-starvation core, compile-only + offline.
2	
3	This is the typed *retrieval view* of a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`
4	(contract + plan). It is the S2 lever the consolidated design (§5, §4 retrieval
5	row) calls out: the gate's scope must reach FS-Researcher **query generation**
6	and backend **routing** BEFORE the first fetch — never as a post-fetch filter of
7	a frozen corpus (the banned 997->131 anti-pattern).
8	
9	A :class:`RetrievalProjection` COMPILES the contract+plan into exactly the three
10	levers ``live_retriever.run_live_retrieval`` already exposes but the driver never
11	populates from the gate:
12	
13	  * ``research_frame``  — a :class:`research_planner.ResearchFrame` whose
14	    ``evidence_needs`` are ROUTED from the contract's ``source_types`` (journal /
15	    academic / scholarly -> ``primary_literature`` = *GO FIND journals*, routing
16	    the S2 / OpenAlex scholarly adapters), plus entities / comparators /
17	    jurisdictions for the field-agnostic need-type registry;
18	  * ``amplified_queries`` — query TEXT built from the plan's MANDATORY query
19	    intents: concepts + required-terms, plus native-language sub-queries for a
20	    hard ``source_languages`` term, plus per-entity sub-queries, plus the hard
21	    scope terms folded into the query text so a "journal-only" rule shapes
22	    discovery from the first query;
23	  * ``protocol`` — the frame's anchor protocol (``to_anchor_protocol``), so the
24	    amplified sub-queries validate against the frame's tokens.
25	
26	Guardrail posture
27	-----------------
28	* **Compile-only. No network, no LLM, no I/O.** This module builds plain data
29	  (lists of strings, a dataclass) from an already-pinned artifact. It NEVER
30	  fetches. The only cross-module import is the champion ``ResearchFrame`` (a pure
31	  dataclass) which it constructs; that construction runs the frame's own
32	  ``validate_evidence_needs`` so a routed need is always a valid enum member.
33	* **ADD / ROUTE, never filter.** Every method returns queries or routing hints
34	  that ENLARGE or STEER discovery. There is no drop path here. The mechanized
35	  997-guard (:func:`candidate_query_count`) counts the candidate query/evidence
36	  lanes so a caller can assert the gate path never REDUCES the lane count vs the
37	  no-gate path — at planning level, before any fetch.
38	* **Hard vs soft.** A HARD scope term (``force==hard``, which the schema
39	  guarantees is explicit/user-backed) is folded into query TEXT and routed to a
40	  backend need. A SOFT term (preference/open) contributes ranking/entity breadth
41	  but is NEVER allowed to gate. This module cannot introduce a hard filter — it
42	  only emits query strings + need tokens.
43	* **Fail-open.** Every builder tolerates a degraded/empty contract: an empty
44	  contract yields an empty projection (no needs, no amplified queries), so the
45	  caller reads the champion default (``research_frame=None`` path) and the run is
46	  byte-identical. Constructing the projection NEVER raises for a shape problem.
47	
48	Nothing here is wired ON by default. ``fs_researcher_query_gen`` /
49	``live_retriever`` / ``run_one_query`` accept a ``retrieval_plan=None`` kwarg
50	(default None => champion behaviour); the projection is threaded only when the
51	``PG_GATE`` flag is set and an artifact exists. See :mod:`gate_flags`.
52	"""
53	
54	from __future__ import annotations
55	
56	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
57	from typing import Any, Optional
58	
59	from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import (
60	    FORCE_HARD,
61	    PlanningGateArtifact,
62	    QueryIntent,
63	    ResearchContract,
64	    ResearchExecutionPlan,
65	)
66	
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	# source_type -> evidence_need routing (the "GO FIND journals" map)
69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	# Maps a contract ``scope.source_types`` value (free-string, model-emitted) onto
71	# the field-agnostic ``EVIDENCE_NEEDS`` enum the need-type router routes adapters
72	# off. This is the load-bearing S2 routing: a "journal articles only" contract
73	# term (task-72) routes ``primary_literature`` = the S2 + OpenAlex + scholarly-
74	# graph adapters, so FS goes and FINDS journals from the first query instead of
75	# filtering a general corpus afterwards.
76	#
77	# Each key is matched as a case-insensitive SUBSTRING of the normalized source
78	# type, so "peer-reviewed journal article", "academic paper", "scholarly
79	# article" all route primary_literature. Unmatched source types contribute no
80	# need (the router's safe generic fallback applies), never an error.
81	_SOURCE_TYPE_NEED_MAP: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
82	    ("journal", "primary_literature"),
83	    ("peer-review", "primary_literature"),
84	    ("peer review", "primary_literature"),
85	    ("academic", "primary_literature"),
86	    ("scholarly", "primary_literature"),
87	    ("scientific", "primary_literature"),
88	    ("paper", "primary_literature"),
89	    ("study", "primary_literature"),
90	    ("preprint", "primary_literature"),
91	    ("regulation", "regulatory"),
92	    ("regulatory", "regulatory"),
93	    ("guideline", "regulatory"),
94	    ("law", "legal"),
95	    ("legal", "legal"),
96	    ("case law", "legal"),
97	    ("statute", "legal"),
98	    ("court", "legal"),
99	    ("statistic", "statistical"),
100	    ("census", "statistical"),
101	    ("survey data", "statistical"),
102	    ("dataset", "datasets"),
103	    ("standard", "standards"),
104	    ("news", "news_press"),
105	    ("press", "news_press"),
106	    ("media", "news_press"),
107	    ("filing", "company_filings"),
108	    ("sec ", "company_filings"),
109	    ("10-k", "company_filings"),
110	    ("annual report", "company_filings"),
111	)
112	
113	
114	def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
115	    return str(s or "").strip().lower()
116	
117	
118	def source_type_to_need(source_type: Any) -> Optional[str]:
119	    """Route a single contract ``source_type`` value onto an ``EVIDENCE_NEEDS``
120	    token, or ``None`` if it matches no known family (the router falls back to
121	    the safe generic set — never an error)."""
122	    st = _norm(source_type)
123	    if not st:
124	        return None
125	    for needle, need in _SOURCE_TYPE_NEED_MAP:
126	        if needle in st:
127	            return need
128	    return None
129	
130	
131	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# The projection
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	
135	
136	@dataclass
137	class RetrievalProjection:
138	    """Compile-time retrieval view of a pinned contract + plan.
139	
140	    Built by :func:`from_artifact` (or :meth:`ResearchContract.to_research_frame`
141	    via the thin methods added to the schema). Holds only plain data; the
142	    ``research_frame`` is materialized lazily by :meth:`to_research_frame` so this
143	    dataclass has no heavy import at construction.
144	    """
145	
146	    contract: ResearchContract
147	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan
148	    original_prompt: str = ""
149	
150	    # Precomputed routing tokens (validated against the frame enum lazily).
151	    evidence_needs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
152	    jurisdictions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
153	    entities: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
154	    comparators: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
155	    # Hard scope-term VALUES (verbatim), folded into query text + surfaced for a
156	    # backend-native filter by a caller that supports one. NEVER a drop here.
157	    hard_scope_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
158	    # Soft/preference scope-term values — ranking hints only, never a gate.
159	    soft_scope_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
160	    # Hard source-language codes/names (e.g. "en"); drive native-language lanes.
161	    hard_languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
162	
163	    # -- amplified query text (the FS frontier seed) --------------------------
164	
165	    def to_amplified_queries(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> list[str]:
166	        """The gate's query TEXT for ``run_live_retrieval(amplified_queries=...)``.
167	
168	        Built from the plan's MANDATORY query intents (concepts + required
169	        terms), each scope-anchored by folding the hard scope terms into the
170	        query string so the FIRST query already carries "journal" / "English"
171	        etc. Then per-entity sub-queries and native-language lanes are ADDED.
172	        Order-stable, de-duplicated (case-insensitively), never truncated here.
173	
174	        This is strictly ADDITIVE: it returns candidate query strings; the caller
175	        (FS-Researcher / live_retriever) runs each through the UNCHANGED
176	        per-query retrieval + frozen faithfulness engine. Nothing is dropped.
177	        """
178	        out: list[str] = []
179	        seen: set[str] = set()
180	
181	        def _add(q: str) -> None:
182	            qs = (q or "").strip()
183	            if not qs:
184	                return
185	            key = qs.lower()
186	            if key in seen:
187	                return
188	            seen.add(key)
189	            out.append(qs)
190	
191	        scope_suffix = " ".join(self.hard_scope_terms).strip()
192	
193	        for qi in self.plan.mandatory_query_intents():
194	            for text in _intent_query_texts(qi):
195	                if scope_suffix and scope_suffix.lower() not in text.lower():
196	                    _add(f"{text} {scope_suffix}")
197	                else:
198	                    _add(text)
199	
200	        # per-entity sub-queries (sub-entity discovery), anchored on the base
201	        # question so a bare entity does not drift into its broad field.
202	        anchor = (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
203	        for ent in self.entities:
204	            ent = (ent or "").strip()
205	            if not ent:
206	                continue
207	            if anchor:
208	                _add(f"{ent} {anchor}")
209	            else:
210	                _add(ent)
211	
212	        # native-language lanes: for a HARD source-language, ADD the scope-anchored
213	        # query in that language slot (the routing hint; the multilingual lane in
214	        # FS translates). We surface a language-tagged variant of the first few
215	        # mandatory concepts so a "source: English-language" rule reaches the
216	        # multilingual reserve as an explicit lane rather than being inferred.
217	        # (English is the pipeline default; a non-English hard language adds a
218	        # native tag so the reserve fires.)
219	        for lang in self.hard_languages:
220	            lang = (lang or "").strip()
221	            if not lang or lang.lower() in ("en", "english"):
222	                continue
223	            for qi in self.plan.mandatory_query_intents()[:2]:
224	                for text in _intent_query_texts(qi):
225	                    _add(f"{text} ({lang})")
226	
227	        return out
228	
229	    # -- scope terms ----------------------------------------------------------
230	
231	    def to_scope_terms(self) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
232	        """The hard/soft scope split + language lanes, as plain lists.
233	
234	        A caller that supports a backend-native filter reads ``hard`` for the
235	        explicit prohibitions/requirements (journal-only, English-only) and
236	        ``soft`` for ranking preferences. This is a compile view — it never
237	        applies a filter itself. The 997-guard reads the union count.
238	        """
239	        return {
240	            "hard": list(self.hard_scope_terms),
241	            "soft": list(self.soft_scope_terms),
242	            "languages": list(self.hard_languages),
243	            "evidence_needs": list(self.evidence_needs),
244	        }
245	
246	    # -- research frame (backend routing) -------------------------------------
247	
248	    def to_research_frame(self) -> Any:
249	        """Materialize a champion :class:`ResearchFrame` for backend routing.
250	
251	        ``evidence_needs`` route the need-type registry (primary_literature ->
252	        S2/OpenAlex scholarly adapters = GO FIND journals). Entities/comparators
253	        seed the anchor tokens. Jurisdictions route jurisdiction scopes. Runs the
254	        frame's own ``validate_evidence_needs`` so a routed need is always a valid
255	        enum member; an unroutable source type simply contributes no need (safe
256	        generic fallback), never an error.
257	
258	        Returns ``None`` when the projection carries NOTHING to route (empty
259	        needs AND no entities/jurisdictions) so the caller keeps the champion
260	        ``research_frame=None`` (byte-identical) path.
261	        """
262	        if not (self.evidence_needs or self.entities or self.jurisdictions
263	                or self.comparators):
264	            return None
265	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import (  # noqa: PLC0415
266	            ResearchFrame,
267	            validate_evidence_needs,
268	            validate_jurisdiction_shapes,
269	        )
270	        # validate/normalize; drop any that fail SHAPE rather than raise (compile
271	        # view is fail-open — a bad token contributes nothing, never aborts).
272	        try:
273	            needs = validate_evidence_needs(self.evidence_needs)
274	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
275	            needs = [n for n in self.evidence_needs
276	                     if _safe_need(n)]
277	        try:
278	            juris = validate_jurisdiction_shapes(self.jurisdictions)
279	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
280	            juris = []
281	        return ResearchFrame(
282	            entities=list(self.entities),
283	            comparators=list(self.comparators),
284	            constraints=list(self.hard_scope_terms),
285	            evidence_needs=needs,
286	            jurisdictions=juris,
287	        )
288	
289	    def to_protocol(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
290	        """The anchor protocol for ``validate_amplified_queries`` (from the frame).
291	
292	        ``None`` when there is no frame to anchor (keeps the champion path)."""
293	        frame = self.to_research_frame()
294	        if frame is None:
295	            return None
296	        anchor = (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
297	        return frame.to_anchor_protocol(anchor)
298	
299	    # -- the mechanized 997-guard (planning level) ----------------------------
300	
301	    def candidate_query_count(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> int:
302	        """Count the candidate query/evidence lanes this projection ADDS.
303	
304	        The 997-guard: a caller asserts ``gate_count >= no_gate_count`` so the
305	        gate path can NEVER reduce the candidate query/evidence-need count vs the
306	        no-gate path — at PLANNING level, before any fetch. Counts distinct
307	        amplified queries plus routed evidence needs (each a discovery lane)."""
308	        return (
309	            len(self.to_amplified_queries(base_question=base_question))
310	            + len(self.evidence_needs)
311	        )
312	
313	
314	def _safe_need(n: Any) -> bool:
315	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import EVIDENCE_NEEDS  # noqa: PLC0415
316	    return _norm(n) in EVIDENCE_NEEDS
317	
318	
319	def _intent_query_texts(qi: QueryIntent) -> list[str]:
320	    """The query string(s) a single :class:`QueryIntent` contributes.
321	
322	    Prefers a concept+required-term join; falls back to alternate phrasings, then
323	    the raw thread question via concepts. Always returns at least one non-empty
324	    string when the intent carries any text."""
325	    parts: list[str] = []
326	    core = " ".join([*qi.concepts, *qi.required_terms]).strip()
327	    if core:
328	        parts.append(core)
329	    for alt in qi.alternate_phrasings:
330	        alt = (alt or "").strip()
331	        if alt:
332	            parts.append(alt)
333	    if not parts:
334	        # nothing structured — fall back to any single concept/phrasing.
335	        for cand in [*qi.concepts, *qi.alternate_phrasings]:
336	            if (cand or "").strip():
337	                parts.append(cand.strip())
338	                break
339	    return parts
340	
341	
342	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
343	# Compilation from a pinned artifact / contract
344	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
345	
346	# Scope dimensions whose VALUES are folded into query text as scope terms.
347	_SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
348	    "scope.source_types",
349	    "scope.domains",
350	    "scope.geographies",
351	    "scope.prohibited",  # a prohibition is disclosed as a term the caller may filter
352	)
353	_LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
354	    "scope.source_languages",
355	    "scope.languages",
356	)
357	
358	
359	def from_contract_and_plan(
360	    contract: ResearchContract,
361	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan,
362	    *,
363	    original_prompt: str = "",
364	) -> RetrievalProjection:
365	    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a contract + plan.
366	
367	    Pure + deterministic. Walks the contract's scope + content terms and the
368	    plan's query intents to derive routing tokens. Fail-open: a degraded/empty
369	    contract yields an empty projection (the caller keeps the champion path).
370	    """
371	    evidence_needs: list[str] = []
372	    jurisdictions: list[str] = []
373	    entities: list[str] = []
374	    comparators: list[str] = []
375	    hard_scope_terms: list[str] = []
376	    soft_scope_terms: list[str] = []
377	    hard_languages: list[str] = []
378	
379	    def _dedup_add(lst: list[str], val: Any) -> None:
380	        s = str(val or "").strip()
381	        if not s:
382	            return
383	        if s.lower() not in {x.lower() for x in lst}:
384	            lst.append(s)
385	
386	    for term in contract.scope:
387	        dim = _norm(term.dimension)
388	        val = term.value
389	        if val in (None, "", [], {}):
390	            continue
391	        vals = val if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)) else [val]
392	
393	        if dim in _LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS:
394	            if term.force == FORCE_HARD:
395	                for v in vals:
396	                    _dedup_add(hard_languages, v)
397	            continue
398	
399	        # source_types route evidence needs (the GO-FIND-journals map).
400	        if dim == "scope.source_types":
401	            for v in vals:
402	                need = source_type_to_need(v)
403	                if need:
404	                    _dedup_add(evidence_needs, need)
405	
406	        if dim in ("scope.jurisdictions", "scope.geographies"):
407	            for v in vals:
408	                _dedup_add(jurisdictions, str(v).strip().upper()[:4])
409	
410	        # fold the value into the hard/soft scope text bucket.
411	        bucket = hard_scope_terms if term.force == FORCE_HARD else soft_scope_terms
412	        if dim in _SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS or term.force == FORCE_HARD:
413	            for v in vals:
414	                _dedup_add(bucket, v)
415	
416	    # entities + comparators from the content spec (sub-entity discovery breadth).
417	    for ent in contract.content_terms:
418	        if _norm(ent.dimension).startswith("content.entit") and ent.value:
419	            _dedup_add(entities, ent.value)
420	    for cov in contract.coverage:
421	        if cov.kind in ("entity",) and cov.statement.value:
422	            _dedup_add(entities, cov.statement.value)
423	        if cov.kind in ("comparison",):
424	            for c in cov.compare_on:
425	                if c.value:
426	                    _dedup_add(comparators, c.value)
427	
428	    return RetrievalProjection(
429	        contract=contract,
430	        plan=plan,
431	        original_prompt=original_prompt,
432	        evidence_needs=evidence_needs,
433	        jurisdictions=jurisdictions,
434	        entities=entities,
435	        comparators=comparators,
436	        hard_scope_terms=hard_scope_terms,
437	        soft_scope_terms=soft_scope_terms,
438	        hard_languages=hard_languages,
439	    )
440	
441	
442	def from_artifact(artifact: PlanningGateArtifact) -> RetrievalProjection:
443	    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a pinned artifact."""
444	    return from_contract_and_plan(
445	        artifact.contract,
446	        artifact.plan,
447	        original_prompt=artifact.original_prompt,
448	    )
449	
450	
451	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
452	# Transitional adapter: champion ResearchPlan -> projection (the FS seam fix)
453	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
454	
455	
456	class _ChampionPlanProjection(RetrievalProjection):
457	    """A projection whose amplified queries ARE the champion plan's ``sub_queries``.
458	
459	    S2 seam fix (design §5): the FS-Researcher branch in ``run_one_query``
460	    currently passes only ``_clean_question`` and DISCARDS
461	    ``_research_plan.sub_queries``. This adapter wraps a champion
462	    ``research_planner.ResearchPlan`` as a :class:`RetrievalProjection` so the FS
463	    frontier is seeded with those sub-queries (the gate's pre-retrieval lanes)
464	    plus the frame's ``evidence_needs`` routing — BEFORE any fetch. Until the full
465	    gate artifact is wired into ``run_one_query`` (a later step), this carries the
466	    already-computed champion plan into the FS query frontier instead of throwing
467	    it away. It NEVER drops a source; it only ADDS the sub-queries the FS branch
468	    was discarding, and only when ``PG_GATE`` is ON.
469	    """
470	
471	    def __init__(self, research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = "") -> None:
472	        frame = getattr(research_plan, "frame", None)
473	        sub_queries = list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or [])
474	        super().__init__(
475	            contract=ResearchContract(),
476	            plan=ResearchExecutionPlan(),
477	            original_prompt=original_prompt,
478	            evidence_needs=list(getattr(frame, "evidence_needs", []) or []),
479	            jurisdictions=list(getattr(frame, "jurisdictions", []) or []),
480	            entities=list(getattr(frame, "entities", []) or []),
481	            comparators=list(getattr(frame, "comparators", []) or []),
482	        )
483	        self._sub_queries = sub_queries
484	        self._frame = frame
485	
486	    def to_amplified_queries(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> list[str]:
487	        out: list[str] = []
488	        seen: set[str] = set()
489	        for q in self._sub_queries:
490	            qs = (q or "").strip()
491	            if qs and qs.lower() not in seen:
492	                seen.add(qs.lower())
493	                out.append(qs)
494	        return out
495	
496	    def to_research_frame(self) -> Any:
497	        return self._frame
498	
499	    def to_protocol(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
500	        if self._frame is None:
501	            return None
502	        try:
503	            return self._frame.to_anchor_protocol(
504	                (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
505	            )
506	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
507	            return None
508	
509	
510	def from_champion_plan(
511	    research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = ""
512	) -> Optional[RetrievalProjection]:
513	    """Adapt a champion ``ResearchPlan`` into a projection, or ``None`` if it has
514	    no usable sub-queries (keep the champion FS path)."""
515	    if research_plan is None:
516	        return None
517	    if not list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or []):
518	        return None
519	    return _ChampionPlanProjection(research_plan, original_prompt=original_prompt)
520

1	"""Outline FEED — the gate ↔ outline-agent seam (S3), additive + default-OFF.
2	
3	The Research Planning Gate produces ONE pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`
4	(contract + plan). At the *post-retrieval* outline stage the gate does not
5	replace the evidence-aware outline agent; it **FEEDS** it (consolidated design
6	§6, Sol §5): it pre-loads the contract's coverage obligations as PENDING gaps so
7	the deep-think loop CLOSES contract gaps, and it threads the retrieval *scope*
8	(the same ``research_frame`` / ``protocol`` used by initial retrieval) into the
9	outline-stage gap search so a correctly scoped first pass is not diluted by an
10	unconstrained follow-up query.
11	
12	This module is PURE + deterministic (no network, no LLM, no I/O). It only reads
13	an already-pinned contract/projection and returns plain data:
14	
15	  * :func:`coverage_gap_seeds` — the contract's REQUIRED coverage obligations
16	    (``required_coverage`` / ``must_address``) as ``(section, aspect)`` gap
17	    seeds. **Required topics are coverage OBLIGATIONS, not automatically
18	    headings** — they are pre-loaded PENDING in the gap ledger, never mapped 1:1
19	    onto section titles (the round-1 coverage-to-heading bug the design forbids).
20	  * :func:`build_term_ledger` — the binding-term → owning-section map + the set
21	    of exact-title / order locks, so ``update_outline`` can reject a revision
22	    that drops an explicit lock or the last owner of a binding term.
23	  * :func:`gate_scope_for_gap_search` — the ``(research_frame, protocol)`` pair a
24	    :class:`RetrievalProjection` compiles, threaded into the outline-stage gap
25	    search (:func:`outline_agent._tool_search_more_evidence`).
26	
27	Guardrail posture
28	-----------------
29	* **ADD / ROUTE, never filter.** The FEED only ADDS PENDING gap todos (search
30	  MORE) and ROUTES the gap search to the gate's scope. It never drops a source,
31	  never filters the banked corpus, never removes a section that owns a binding
32	  term. There is no drop path here.
33	* **Coverage != heading.** Required coverage becomes a gap OBLIGATION with an
34	  ``(section="", aspect=<topic>)`` unassigned home, so the loop searches for it
35	  and the outliner homes it under whatever section fits — the topic is never
36	  forced to become its own heading.
37	* **Fail-open.** Every helper tolerates a degraded/empty/``None`` contract or
38	  projection: it returns empty data, so the caller reads the champion default
39	  (no seeded gaps, no ledger locks, no scoped gap search) and the run is
40	  byte-identical to today's champion.
41	* **Never invent a constraint.** Only ``force == hard`` terms (which the schema
42	  guarantees are ``origin == explicit``/user-backed) become binding-term ledger
43	  owners or locks. Inferred / preference terms contribute optional gap seeds
44	  only — never a lock, never a hard veto on a revision.
45	"""
46	
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	from typing import Any, Optional
50	
51	# Keep this module import-light: the schema types are only used for isinstance /
52	# attribute reads, all guarded, so a caller may pass duck-typed objects too.
53	
54	# Coverage-requirement dimensions/keys that name a REQUIRED obligation. A gate
55	# contract carries these as ``CoverageRequirement`` objects; a looser upstream
56	# may hand a plain dict with ``required_coverage`` / ``must_address`` lists.
57	_REQUIRED_COVERAGE_KEYS: tuple[str, ...] = ("required_coverage", "must_address")
58	
59	
60	def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
61	    return str(s or "").strip()
62	
63	
64	def _term_value_text(term: Any) -> str:
65	    """The human-readable text of a ContractTerm-ish object (or plain value)."""
66	    if term is None:
67	        return ""
68	    val = getattr(term, "value", term)
69	    if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)):
70	        return " ".join(_norm(v) for v in val if _norm(v)).strip()
71	    return _norm(val)
72	
73	
74	def _is_hard(term: Any) -> bool:
75	    """True iff the term is a HARD force term (schema guarantees explicit/user)."""
76	    force = getattr(term, "force", None)
77	    if force is not None:
78	        return str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"
79	    # duck-typed dict shape
80	    if isinstance(term, dict):
81	        return str(term.get("force", "")).strip().lower() == "hard"
82	    return False
83	
84	
85	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	# FEED 1 — coverage obligations -> PENDING gap seeds (NOT headings)
87	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
88	
89	
90	def coverage_gap_seeds(contract: Any) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
91	    """The contract's REQUIRED coverage obligations as gap seeds.
92	
93	    Returns a list of ``{"section": ..., "aspect": ..., "term_id": ...}``. Each
94	    is a coverage OBLIGATION the deep-think loop should CLOSE — pre-loaded PENDING
95	    in the gap ledger by the caller, so ``next_pending()`` routes a scoped gap
96	    search for it. **The ``section`` is deliberately left EMPTY ("(unassigned)")**
97	    unless the coverage requirement is bound to an EXPLICIT section: a required
98	    topic is a coverage obligation, not automatically a heading (the round-1 bug).
99	
100	    Fail-open: a ``None`` / empty / shapeless contract yields ``[]`` (no seeds),
101	    so the caller keeps the champion behavior (no pre-loaded gaps).
102	    """
103	    if contract is None:
104	        return []
105	    out: list[dict[str, str]] = []
106	    seen: set[str] = set()
107	
108	    def _add(aspect: str, term_id: str = "", section: str = "") -> None:
109	        aspect = _norm(aspect)
110	        if not aspect:
111	            return
112	        key = f"{_norm(section).lower()}||{aspect.lower()}"
113	        if key in seen:
114	            return
115	        seen.add(key)
116	        out.append({
117	            "section": _norm(section),
118	            "aspect": aspect,
119	            "term_id": _norm(term_id),
120	        })
121	
122	    # 1) Structured contract: CoverageRequirement objects marked required.
123	    coverage = getattr(contract, "coverage", None)
124	    if isinstance(coverage, (list, tuple)):
125	        # map requirement_id -> the explicit section that owns it (if any), so a
126	        # coverage req the deliverable EXPLICITLY bound to a section keeps that
127	        # home; every other required topic stays unassigned (never a new heading).
128	        req_to_section = _explicit_req_sections(contract)
129	        for cr in coverage:
130	            required = getattr(cr, "required", True)
131	            if not required:
132	                continue
133	            aspect = _term_value_text(getattr(cr, "statement", None))
134	            if not aspect:
135	                # fall back to the requirement's own kind/id so it is still tracked
136	                aspect = _norm(getattr(cr, "requirement_id", ""))
137	            req_id = _norm(getattr(cr, "requirement_id", ""))
138	            term_id = _norm(getattr(getattr(cr, "statement", None), "term_id", "")) or req_id
139	            _add(aspect, term_id=term_id, section=req_to_section.get(req_id, ""))
140	
141	    # 2) Duck-typed dict shape (a looser upstream): required_coverage/must_address.
142	    if isinstance(contract, dict):
143	        for key in _REQUIRED_COVERAGE_KEYS:
144	            vals = contract.get(key)
145	            if isinstance(vals, (list, tuple)):
146	                for v in vals:
147	                    _add(_term_value_text(v) or _norm(v))
148	
149	    return out
150	
151	
152	def _explicit_req_sections(contract: Any) -> dict[str, str]:
153	    """Map ``requirement_id -> explicit section title`` for coverage reqs the
154	    deliverable EXPLICITLY placed under an exact-title-locked section. Only an
155	    explicit / user section counts (never an inferred/gate-proposed heading), so
156	    a required topic never silently becomes a heading."""
157	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
158	    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
159	    if not isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
160	        return out
161	    for sec in sections:
162	        if not getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
163	            continue
164	        title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
165	        if not title:
166	            continue
167	        for rid in (getattr(sec, "coverage_requirement_ids", None) or []):
168	            rid = _norm(rid)
169	            if rid:
170	                out[rid] = title
171	    return out
172	
173	
174	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
175	# FEED 2 — the term ledger (binding term -> owning sections + locks)
176	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
177	
178	
179	def build_term_ledger(
180	    contract: Any, coverage_matrix: Any = None,
181	) -> dict[str, Any]:
182	    """Build the immutable term ledger the outline workspace validates against.
183	
184	    Returns a plain dict::
185	
186	        {
187	          "binding_term_owners": {term_id: [section_title, ...]},  # last-owner guard
188	          "locked_titles": [title, ...],       # exact-title locks (explicit/user)
189	          "ordered_titles": [title, ...],      # order-locked titles (in order)
190	          "binding_term_ids": [term_id, ...],  # every hard/binding term id
191	        }
192	
193	    Only HARD (``force == hard``, schema-guaranteed explicit/user) terms are
194	    binding. Section ownership is read from the plan's ``coverage_matrix`` when
195	    supplied (a binding term -> its ``section_ids``); a section that owns a
196	    binding term is protected from being dropped as the LAST owner.
197	
198	    Fail-open: ``None`` / empty contract -> an empty ledger (no locks, no binding
199	    owners), so ``update_outline`` validation is a no-op and the run is
200	    byte-identical.
201	    """
202	    ledger: dict[str, Any] = {
203	        "binding_term_owners": {},
204	        "locked_titles": [],
205	        "ordered_titles": [],
206	        "binding_term_ids": [],
207	    }
208	    if contract is None:
209	        return ledger
210	
211	    # binding term ids: every HARD term across the contract.
212	    binding_ids: list[str] = []
213	    all_terms = getattr(contract, "all_terms", None)
214	    if callable(all_terms):
215	        try:
216	            for t in all_terms():
217	                if _is_hard(t):
218	                    tid = _norm(getattr(t, "term_id", ""))
219	                    if tid:
220	                        binding_ids.append(tid)
221	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
222	            binding_ids = []
223	    ledger["binding_term_ids"] = _dedup(binding_ids)
224	
225	    # exact-title / order locks: explicit section requirements.
226	    locked: list[str] = []
227	    ordered: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
228	    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
229	    if isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
230	        for sec in sections:
231	            title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
232	            if not title:
233	                continue
234	            if getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
235	                locked.append(title)
236	            order = getattr(sec, "order", None)
237	            if isinstance(order, int) and getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
238	                ordered.append((order, title))
239	    ledger["locked_titles"] = _dedup(locked)
240	    ledger["ordered_titles"] = [t for _, t in sorted(ordered, key=lambda x: x[0])]
241	
242	    # binding-term -> owning sections (from the coverage matrix). Only bindings
243	    # whose term id is in binding_ids get last-owner protection.
244	    owners: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
245	    matrix = coverage_matrix
246	    if matrix is None:
247	        matrix = getattr(getattr(contract, "plan", None), "coverage_matrix", None)
248	    if isinstance(matrix, (list, tuple)):
249	        binding_set = set(ledger["binding_term_ids"])
250	        # map requirement/section ids to section TITLES via the contract sections.
251	        sec_id_to_title = _section_id_to_title(contract)
252	        for binding in matrix:
253	            tid = _norm(getattr(binding, "contract_term_id", ""))
254	            if tid and tid not in binding_set:
255	                # the coverage-matrix may bind a coverage requirement whose
256	                # statement term is hard; keep only genuinely-binding ids.
257	                continue
258	            sec_ids = getattr(binding, "section_ids", None) or []
259	            titles = [
260	                sec_id_to_title.get(_norm(sid), _norm(sid))
261	                for sid in sec_ids if _norm(sid)
262	            ]
263	            titles = [t for t in titles if t]
264	            if tid and titles:
265	                owners.setdefault(tid, [])
266	                for t in titles:
267	                    if t not in owners[tid]:
268	                        owners[tid].append(t)
269	    ledger["binding_term_owners"] = owners
270	    return ledger
271	
272	
273	def _section_id_to_title(contract: Any) -> dict[str, str]:
274	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
275	    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
276	    if isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
277	        for sec in sections:
278	            sid = _norm(getattr(sec, "section_id", ""))
279	            title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
280	            if sid and title:
281	                out[sid] = title
282	    return out
283	
284	
285	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	# FEED 3 — the gate's retrieval scope for the outline-stage gap search
287	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
288	
289	
290	def gate_scope_for_gap_search(
291	    projection: Any, *, base_question: str = "",
292	) -> tuple[Any, Optional[dict[str, Any]]]:
293	    """The ``(research_frame, protocol)`` a :class:`RetrievalProjection` compiles.
294	
295	    THE BUG FIX seam (both designers): ``outline_agent._tool_search_more_evidence``
296	    calls ``run_live_retrieval`` WITHOUT ``research_frame`` / ``protocol`` today, so
297	    the outline-stage gap search runs UNSCOPED even under a hard journal / language
298	    / jurisdiction contract. This returns the SAME scope levers the initial
299	    retrieval used, so the gap query keeps that scope.
300	
301	    Fail-open: a ``None`` projection (or one that routes nothing) yields
302	    ``(None, None)`` — exactly the champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults, so the
303	    gap search is byte-identical to today.
304	    """
305	    if projection is None:
306	        return (None, None)
307	    frame = None
308	    protocol = None
309	    to_frame = getattr(projection, "to_research_frame", None)
310	    if callable(to_frame):
311	        try:
312	            frame = to_frame()
313	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
314	            frame = None
315	    to_proto = getattr(projection, "to_protocol", None)
316	    if callable(to_proto):
317	        try:
318	            protocol = to_proto(base_question=base_question)
319	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
320	            protocol = None
321	    return (frame, protocol)
322	
323	
324	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
325	# update_outline validation — reject dropping a lock / last binding owner
326	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
327	
328	
329	def validate_revision_against_ledger(
330	    ledger: Optional[dict[str, Any]],
331	    *,
332	    titles_before: list[str],
333	    titles_after: list[str],
334	) -> list[str]:
335	    """Return a list of VIOLATION reasons if the revision drops an explicit lock or
336	    the last owner of a binding term. Empty list => the revision is allowed.
337	
338	    ``titles_before`` / ``titles_after`` are the section title lists before and
339	    after the proposed op set. A violation is:
340	
341	      * a ``locked_title`` present before but absent after (explicit title lock
342	        dropped), OR
343	      * a binding term whose EVERY owning section is absent after (the last owner
344	        of a binding requirement dropped).
345	
346	    Fail-open: a ``None`` / empty ledger => ``[]`` (no constraint), byte-identical.
347	    A retitle that keeps the same count but renames a locked title IS a violation
348	    (the locked title disappeared) — the caller may then defer the op.
349	    """
350	    if not ledger:
351	        return []
352	    before = {_norm(t) for t in (titles_before or []) if _norm(t)}
353	    after = {_norm(t) for t in (titles_after or []) if _norm(t)}
354	    violations: list[str] = []
355	
356	    for locked in (ledger.get("locked_titles") or []):
357	        locked_n = _norm(locked)
358	        if locked_n in before and locked_n not in after:
359	            violations.append(f"explicit_title_lock_dropped:{locked_n!r}")
360	
361	    owners: dict[str, list[str]] = ledger.get("binding_term_owners") or {}
362	    for tid, sec_titles in owners.items():
363	        present_before = [t for t in sec_titles if _norm(t) in before]
364	        if not present_before:
365	            # the binding term had no owner present before — nothing to protect.
366	            continue
367	        present_after = [t for t in sec_titles if _norm(t) in after]
368	        if not present_after:
369	            violations.append(f"last_owner_of_binding_term_dropped:{tid!r}")
370	
371	    return violations
372	
373	
374	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
375	# small utils
376	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
377	
378	
379	def _dedup(items: list[str]) -> list[str]:
380	    out: list[str] = []
381	    seen: set[str] = set()
382	    for it in items:
383	        it = _norm(it)
384	        if it and it.lower() not in seen:
385	            seen.add(it.lower())
386	            out.append(it)
387	    return out
388

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""STEP 15: compose the REAL scoreable multi-section report from the LIVE agentic run.
3	
4	The mission's metric-(a) full-corpus gate (cp4_used=agentic on the 329-basket corpus) already
5	PASSED (docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json). What was missing was a *composed*
6	report we can score. This driver closes that gap: it runs the FULL generator
7	(``generate_multi_section_report``) with the agentic outliner ON (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the
8	§9.1.8 model lock) over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json and writes report.md.
9	
10	Model it on scripts/run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py (which already produced report.md +
11	multi_section_outline.json), minus the retrieval/scope machinery (the corpus is pre-built).
12	
13	Faithfulness gate (HARD): after composition, assert ZERO unverified numbers reach any
14	[CITE:ev_xxx] token in the composed report. The strict_verify lane already enforces this
15	per-section; this driver re-audits the final assembled text as an independent tripwire.
16	
17	Run (key MUST be in env):
18	    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
19	    PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py \
20	        --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import argparse
25	import asyncio
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import os
29	import re
30	import sys
31	import time
32	from pathlib import Path
33	from types import SimpleNamespace
34	
35	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
36	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
37	
38	DRB_QUERY = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench" / "data" / "prompt_data" / "query.jsonl"
39	
40	# STEP 2 (wheel: topic-driven structure) — the section headings are now produced TOPIC-DRIVEN by
41	# the generator itself (facet outline + general research-report skeleton: PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 +
42	# PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1). The prior STEP-16 approach hardcoded a clinical-archetype ->
43	# AI/labor relabel MAP here — an overfit band-aid tuned to one benchmark task. That map is GONE:
44	# the outliner emits real topical titles (Introduction / thematic bodies / Cross-Study Synthesis /
45	# Conclusions and Research Gaps) for ANY domain, so assembly renders the section titles verbatim.
46	
47	
48	def _derive_title(rq: str) -> str:
49	    """Derive a neutral report title from the research question — GENERAL, not tuned to any task.
50	
51	    Takes the first sentence/clause of the RQ, strips a leading imperative ("Please write a ...",
52	    "Research ...", "I am researching ..."), and Title-cases nothing (keeps the RQ's own wording).
53	    Falls back to a generic label. No topic is hardcoded."""
54	    import re as _re
55	    s = (rq or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
56	    s = _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s)
57	    # First sentence only.
58	    s = _re.split(r"(?<=[.?!])\s", s, maxsplit=1)[0]
59	    # Strip common leading imperatives so the title reads as a subject, not a command.
60	    s = _re.sub(r"^(please\s+)?(help me\s+)?(write|prepare|produce|conduct|research(ing)?|"
61	                r"provide|create|complete|collect( and)?( organi[sz]e)?|i am researching|"
62	                r"i would like|i need)\b[:,]?\s*", "", s, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)
63	    s = s.strip().rstrip(".").strip()
64	    if not s:
65	        return "Research Report"
66	    # Capitalize the first letter only (preserve proper-noun casing in the rest).
67	    return s[0].upper() + s[1:]
68	
69	
70	def _load_drb_prompt(task_id: str) -> str:
71	    """Load a DeepResearch-Bench task's EXACT prompt verbatim (target/ref/criteria all key on it)."""
72	    for line in DRB_QUERY.read_text().splitlines():
73	        o = json.loads(line)
74	        if str(o.get("id")) == str(task_id):
75	            return o["prompt"]
76	    raise SystemExit(f"BLOCKED: DRB task id {task_id} not in {DRB_QUERY}")
77	
78	logging.basicConfig(
79	    level=os.environ.get("PG_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"),
80	    format="%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
81	    datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
82	    stream=sys.stdout,
83	)
84	for noisy in ("httpx", "httpcore"):
85	    logging.getLogger(noisy).setLevel(logging.WARNING)
86	log = logging.getLogger("compose")
87	
88	
89	def _tier_fractions(evidence: list[dict]) -> dict[str, float]:
90	    from collections import Counter
91	    c = Counter((e.get("tier") or "T?").upper() for e in evidence)
92	    n = sum(c.values()) or 1
93	    return {k: v / n for k, v in sorted(c.items())}
94	
95	
96	# A numeric token that would be a faithfulness breach if it sat inside a [CITE:] sentence
97	# without having passed strict_verify. We audit the FINAL assembled report: any [CITE:ev_xxx]
98	# in the verified text is, by construction, already span-grounded — but we re-scan to prove it.
99	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[CITE:(ev_[0-9a-fA-F]+|[a-z0-9_]+)\]")
100	
101	
102	def _audit_citations(report_text: str, biblio: list[dict]) -> dict:
103	    """Independent faithfulness tripwire on the FINAL assembled report.
104	
105	    strict_verify resolves every kept sentence's provenance token into a global [N] bibliography
106	    marker and DROPS any sentence whose number failed the span match. So in a faithful final
107	    report: (1) ZERO raw [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens survive (any survivor is an unverified-number leak
108	    — the exact breach the mission forbids), and (2) every [N] marker in the prose resolves to a
109	    real bibliography entry. We assert both."""
110	    leaked_cites = _CITE_RE.findall(report_text)
111	    body = report_text.split("\n\n## References\n", 1)[0]  # markers in prose only
112	    n_markers = set(int(m) for m in re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", body))
113	    biblio_nums = {int(b.get("num")) for b in biblio if str(b.get("num", "")).isdigit()}
114	    unresolved = sorted(n for n in n_markers if n not in biblio_nums)
115	    return {
116	        "leaked_cite_ev_tokens": len(leaked_cites),
117	        "leaked_cite_samples": sorted(set(leaked_cites))[:10],
118	        "distinct_bib_markers_in_prose": len(n_markers),
119	        "bibliography_entries": len(biblio_nums),
120	        "unresolved_markers": unresolved,
121	    }
122	
123	
124	async def main() -> int:
125	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
126	    ap.add_argument("--corpus", required=True)
127	    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", default=None)
128	    ap.add_argument("--max-parallel", type=int, default=3)
129	    ap.add_argument("--rq-drb-task", default="72",
130	                    help="override the corpus RQ with this DRB task's verbatim prompt so the "
131	                         "composed report answers the SAME task it is scored against; empty string "
132	                         "keeps the corpus RQ")
133	    ap.add_argument("--title", default=None,
134	                    help="report title for the judged report.md; default DERIVES it from the RQ "
135	                         "(general — no title is hardcoded to any task)")
136	    args = ap.parse_args()
137	
138	    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
139	        log.error("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not in env — source .env first "
140	                  "(set -a && . ./.env && set +a)")
141	        return 2
142	    # The mission model-lock: agentic outliner ON.
143	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT", "1")
144	    # P0 CONFIRMED-SAFE COMPOSE CONFIG (2026-07-12) — PIN the non-deadlocking config in the launch
145	    # path. The clean 24.2min/1449.7s run used exactly this: off-loop ON (shipped, verdict-safe),
146	    # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1 (serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE — NEVER >1 without a full-328
147	    # verdict-identity A/B), PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY in the 4-8 band (NEVER >=48), and
148	    # PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3. These are setdefault (an explicit operator override still wins) but they
149	    # keep this driver on the certified-safe path; the startup guard (compose_config_guard) refuses the
150	    # deadlocking regime regardless. Faithfulness-neutral: pure concurrency knobs.
151	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS", "1")
152	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY", "8")
153	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS", "3")
154	    # P1-SPEED (2026-07-12) — collapse the ISOLATED pre-compose credibility member-verify pass.
155	    # ROOT-CAUSE of the 43min (2589.7s) >> 24min (1449.7s) gap, MEASURED from the phase timeline in
156	    # logs/step3_full328_render.log: threading the PSL gov_suffixes (below) to lift route_all basket
157	    # utilization ALSO activates the ADVISORY credibility corroboration pass. On this 997-member corpus
158	    # that pass ran SERIALLY (PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT default=1) and BANKED at its
159	    # wall*0.85 soft deadline = 1020s, verifying only 207/997 members — a full +1020s phase the 1449.7s
160	    # baseline NEVER ran (it did not thread gov_suffixes -> credibility degraded-to-unscored, skipped).
161	    # This pass runs ENTIRELY BEFORE compose (an ISOLATED flat phase — NO PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS x
162	    # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS x inner-TPE nesting), so bounding its OWN loop concurrency is NOT the
163	    # multiplicative compose oversubscription the deadlock guard protects against. Parallelize the
164	    # member-verify loop and raise the side-judge cap FOR THIS PHASE ONLY (the designed I-deepfix-001
165	    # box2 lever; credibility_pass_concurrency RESTORES the compose-time cap of 8 before compose starts).
166	    # Faithfulness-neutral & UNDERCOUNT-only: the pass is ADVISORY (strict_verify / 4-role D8 /
167	    # span-grounding are untouched); verifying MORE members in LESS time yields STRICTLY MORE
168	    # corroboration than the 207-serial run and far more than the baseline's zero. All env-overridable.
169	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT", "16")
170	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_SIDE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY", "16")
171	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_WALL_S", "600")
172	    # STEP 2: topic-driven, synthesis-enabling structure. Facet outline (thematic sections emerge
173	    # from the evidence) + the general research-report skeleton (intro / thematic bodies /
174	    # cross-study synthesis+contradictions / conclusions+gaps). GENERAL structural flags — they
175	    # hardcode no topic and are overridable from the environment.
176	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE", "1")
177	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON", "1")
178	    # STEP 3 (INSIGHT depth): make the cross-study synthesis section quantify agreement/disagreement
179	    # across the [ev]-backed body figures (enrich its evidence + directive). GENERAL structural
180	    # lever — role detected structurally, no topic/title hardcoded; strict_verify unchanged.
181	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE", "1")
182	    # STEP 4 (UTILIZATION — the basket under-utilization ghost): the live LLM outline lists only a
183	    # handful of ev_ids per section, so ~90% of the consolidated baskets reach NO section and never
184	    # compose a cited claim (measured 31/329 rendered; scripts/measure_utilization_route_all.py). Route
185	    # every ORPHAN basket to its best-matching thematic section by claim-vs-title content overlap (else a
186	    # single keep-all residual section). GENERAL, faithfulness-neutral: pure CONSOLIDATE placement —
187	    # drops no source, caps nothing; every routed basket's rendered sentence re-passes the UNCHANGED
188	    # strict_verify per clause. Deterministic A/B proved 31->328 baskets rendered. Also drop the
189	    # PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION row-cap ceiling so a facet keeps its full matched payload.
190	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS", "1")
191	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD", "1")
192	
193	    corpus_path = Path(args.corpus)
194	    corpus = json.loads(corpus_path.read_text())
195	    corpus_rq = corpus["research_question"]
196	    if args.rq_drb_task:
197	        rq = _load_drb_prompt(args.rq_drb_task)
198	        log.info("RQ OVERRIDE: composing to DRB task %s verbatim prompt (corpus RQ kept as "
199	                 "provenance only). task_rq[:90]=%r", args.rq_drb_task, rq[:90])
200	    else:
201	        rq = corpus_rq
202	    evidence = corpus["evidence"]
203	    raw_clusters = corpus.get("finding_clusters") or []
204	    clusters = [SimpleNamespace(**c) if isinstance(c, dict) else c for c in raw_clusters]
205	    swg = corpus.get("same_work_groups")
206	    domain = corpus.get("domain", "")
207	
208	    run_id = time.strftime("agentic_report_%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
209	    run_dir = ROOT / (args.out_dir or f"outputs/{run_id}")
210	    run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
211	    log.info("corpus=%s  evidence=%d  clusters=%d  same_work_groups=%s  domain=%s",
212	             corpus_path.name, len(evidence), len(clusters),
213	             len(swg or []), domain or "(none)")
214	    log.info("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=%s  out_dir=%s", os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT"), run_dir)
215	
216	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
217	        generate_multi_section_report,
218	    )
219	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
220	        PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL, PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
221	    )
222	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import (  # noqa: PLC0415
223	        outliner_agent_model, outliner_code_model,
224	    )
225	
226	    dist = _tier_fractions(evidence)
227	    log.info("tier fractions: %s", {k: round(v, 3) for k, v in dist.items()})
228	    log.info("[gen] agent_model=%s code_model=%s generator=%s",
229	             outliner_agent_model(), outliner_code_model(), PG_GENERATOR_MODEL)
230	
231	    # STEP 4 (UTILIZATION): thread the PSL government-suffix list so the credibility pass RUNS
232	    # priors-only (judge=None under always-release => ZERO LLM scoring calls) and BUILDS the per-claim
233	    # baskets. Without gov_suffixes the pre-run guard DEGRADES to credibility_analysis=None (the
234	    # 794->9 collapse), which strands EVERY basket and makes PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS inert — the report
235	    # then renders only the LLM-writer's directly-cited sources. Faithfulness-neutral: priors weights
236	    # are deterministic authority weights; strict_verify / 4-role D8 / span-grounding stay the ONLY
237	    # binding gates. Fail-open: an empty/unavailable suffix list leaves the legacy None path.
238	    _gov_suffixes = None
239	    try:
240	        from src.polaris_graph.authority.data_loader import load_authority_data  # noqa: PLC0415
241	        _gov_suffixes = tuple(load_authority_data().get("psl_gov_suffixes") or ()) or None
242	        log.info("[credibility] threaded psl_gov_suffixes=%d (priors-only basket build enabled)",
243	                 len(_gov_suffixes or ()))
244	    except Exception as _e:  # noqa: BLE001
245	        log.warning("[credibility] could not load psl_gov_suffixes (%s); credibility pass will "
246	                    "degrade to None and PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS will be inert", _e)
247	
248	    t0 = time.time()
249	    multi = await generate_multi_section_report(
250	        research_question=rq,
251	        evidence=evidence,
252	        finding_clusters=clusters,
253	        same_work_groups=swg,
254	        section_temperature=0.3,
255	        outline_max_tokens=2500,

259	        tier_fractions=dist,

261	        credibility_pass_gov_suffixes=_gov_suffixes,
262	    )
263	    dt = time.time() - t0
264	    kept = [s for s in multi.sections if not s.dropped_due_to_failure]
265	    log.info("[gen] elapsed=%.1fs  outline=%d sections  kept=%d  words=%s  "
266	             "verified=%s  dropped=%s  in_tok=%s out_tok=%s",
267	             dt, len(multi.outline), len(kept), getattr(multi, "total_words", "?"),
268	             getattr(multi, "total_sentences_verified", "?"),
269	             getattr(multi, "total_sentences_dropped", "?"),
270	             getattr(multi, "total_input_tokens", "?"),
271	             getattr(multi, "total_output_tokens", "?"))
272	    for sr in multi.sections:
273	        mark = "OK " if not sr.dropped_due_to_failure else "DROP"
274	        log.info("   [%s] %-42s verified=%s dropped=%s regen=%s",
275	                 mark, sr.title[:42], sr.sentences_verified,
276	                 sr.sentences_dropped, sr.regen_attempted)
277	
278	    # Persist the outline
